from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Helper to clean quoted objects
        def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
            return o.strip('"').strip("'")

        clauses = []
        handled = set()

        # Process location first to possibly add a relative clause about the location entity
        location_triples = [tr for tr in group if tr.predicate == "location"]
        for loc in location_triples:
            loc_obj = clean_obj(loc.object)
            extra_parts = []
            for extra_tr in subject_groups.get(loc_obj, []):
                if extra_tr.predicate in {"leader", "currency", "capital", "language"}:
                    verb = verb_map.get(extra_tr.predicate, extra_tr.predicate)
                    if verb.startswith("leader"):
                        phrase = f"leader is {clean_obj(extra_tr.object)}"
                    elif verb.startswith("currency"):
                        phrase = f"currency is {clean_obj(extra_tr.object)}"
                    elif verb.startswith("capital"):
                        phrase = f"capital is {clean_obj(extra_tr.object)}"
                    elif verb.startswith("language"):
                        phrase = f"language is {clean_obj(extra_tr.object)}"
                    else:
                        phrase = f"{verb} {clean_obj(extra_tr.object)}"
                    extra_parts.append(phrase)
            if extra_parts:
                clause = f"is located in {loc_obj}, whose " + ", ".join(extra_parts)
            else:
                clause = f"is located in {loc_obj}"
            clauses.append(clause)
            handled.add("location")

        # Handle other predicates, including special inverted cases
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            if tr.predicate == "capital":
                clauses.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
                continue
            if tr.predicate == "language":
                clauses.append(f"{clean_obj(tr.object)} is the language of {subject}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
                continue
            if tr.predicate == "leader":
                clauses.append(f"{clean_obj(tr.object)} is the leader of {subject}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
                continue
            if tr.predicate == "buildingStartDate":
                clauses.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
                continue
            if tr.predicate == "inaugurationDate":
                clauses.append(f"The inauguration of {subject} was on {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
                continue

            verb = verb_map.get(tr.predicate, "has")
            obj = clean_obj(tr.object)
            if tr.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} floors"
            else:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            clauses.append(clause)

        # Build sentence for this subject
        if clauses:
            sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(clauses) + "."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences for multiple subjects.
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output.
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
